Package org.eventb.core.ast

Interface IFormulaRewriter

All Known Subinterfaces:
IFormulaRewriter2
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractRewriterImpl, AndOrDistRewriterImpl, ArithRewriterImpl, AutoRewriterImpl, BoundIdentifierDecomposer, CompUnionDistRewriterImpl, ContImplRewriter, ConvRewriterImpl, DefaultRewriter, DisjunctionToImplicationRewriter, DomCompRewriterImpl, DomDistLeftRewriterImpl, DomDistRightRewriterImpl, DomRanUnionDistRewriterImpl, DoubleImplicationRewriter, EqvRewriterImpl, FormulaIndexer.BoundRewriter, HideOriginalRewrites.HideOriginalRewriter, ImpAndRewriterImpl, ImpOrRewriterImpl, InclusionSetMinusLeftRewriterImpl, InclusionSetMinusRightRewriterImpl, Lib.EqualityRewriter, Lib.EquivalenceRewriter, Lib.FixedRewriter, PartitionRewriterImpl, PredicateSimplifier, RanCompRewriterImpl, RanDistLeftRewriterImpl, RanDistRightRewriterImpl, RelImgUnionLeftRewriterImpl, RelImgUnionRightRewriterImpl, RelOvrRewriterImpl, RemoveInclusionRewriterImpl, RemoveInclusionUniversalRewriterImpl, RemoveMembershipRewriterImpl, RemoveNegationRewriterImpl, SetEqlRewriterImpl, SetMinusRewriterImpl, StrictInclusionRewriterImpl, TestSubFormulas.FixedRewriter, TestSubFormulas.OldRewriter, TypeRewriterImpl, UnionInterDistRewriterImpl
public interface IFormulaRewriter
Common protocol for formula rewriters. A formula rewriter implements rewriting of sub-formulas of some formula. This protocol does not support predicate variables. See IFormulaRewriter2.

This interface contains one method for each of the sub-classes of Formula, except assignments which are not covered by sub-formula rewriting.

Rewriting must be compatible with type-checking. For each rewrite method, if the input formula is type-checked, then the returned formula must also be type-checked. Moreover, if the input formula is a type-checked expression, then the returned expression must bear the same mathematical type.

Rewriting must return nodes with the same formula factory as original nodes. Formula factory modification can only be done with the Formula.translate(FormulaFactory) procedure.

Implementation note: If a rewrite method does not make any change to a sub-formula, it should return a formula identical to its input (same reference). This makes it much easier for testing whether a rewriter made any change to a formula.

Clients may implement this interface.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • Formula.rewrite(IFormulaRewriter)
  • DefaultRewriter

  • Method Details

    • autoFlatteningMode

      boolean autoFlatteningMode()
      Tells whether rewrites should be automatically flattened. When this method returns true, the result of rewriting is automatically flattened when inserted in the new formula. The flattening is done only at the point of insertion. No attempt is done to flatten the sub-formulas returned by this rewriter.

      If this mode is turned on and if all the sub-formulas returned by the rewrite methods are flattened, then the rewritten formula is flattened. Moreover, all sub-formulas passed as argument to the rewrite methods are flattened.

      To summarize, if this mode is turned on and the rewriter doesn't introduce itself new sub-formulas which are not flattened, then the result formula is flattened.

      Returns:
      true iff rewritten formulas should be flattened
      See Also:
      • Formula.flatten()

    • enteringQuantifier

      void enteringQuantifier(int nbOfDeclarations)
      Rewriting is entering a quantified formula. When traversing the formula tree, this method is called when the rewriting traverses a quantifier, while going down the tree. In other term, this method is called just before rewriting the children of a quantified formula.

      This method is especially handful if the rewriting manipulates some non-closed formula, the bound identifiers of which need to be shifted so that they retain the index corresponding to their declaration.

      This method may be empty in case the rewriting is always performed on a local basis (such as replacing a formula by an equivalent but simpler one).

      Parameters:
      nbOfDeclarations - nb of bound identifiers declared
      See Also:
      • Formula.shiftBoundIdentifiers(int)
      • leavingQuantifier(int)

    • leavingQuantifier

      void leavingQuantifier(int nbOfDeclarations)
      Rewriting is leaving a quantifier. When traversing the formula tree, this method is called when the rewriting traverses a quantifier, while going back up the tree. In other term, this method is called just after rewriting the children of a quantified formula, and just before rewriting the quantified formula itself.

      This method may be empty in case the rewriting is always performed on a local basis (such as replacing a formula by an equivalent but simpler one).

      Parameters:
      nbOfDeclarations - nb of bound identifiers declared
      See Also:
      • Formula.shiftBoundIdentifiers(int)
      • enteringQuantifier(int)
